fields.description:
## Why
The rising floor's remediation-mode eligibility (src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py:182 remediation_mode_candidate) requires a prior CERTIFIED plan-review signature ("signed"). A BLOCK never signs, so the floor is structurally inert in BLOCK loops - the regime driving 76% of extra review rounds. Post-flip field data: 287/382 plan reviews ineligible (240 with signed=False as the first failing precondition), 33 eligible, 0 novelty drops ever. The floor only drops advisories, but BLOCK-loop advisory walls balloon (ticket 36c7: 21 -> 36 advisories over 10 rounds), so engaging it there is real relief.

## What
- Persist the baseline signals in the sidecar: `build_payload` in src/rebar/llm/plan_review/sidecar.py gains two additive optional fields - `verified_at_sha` (the review-time code SHA, sourced from the same value the orchestrator stamps on the verdict/attestation path, falling back to rebar.llm.config.current_code_sha() at emit) and `regver` (the criteria-registry version at review time). Both are optional on the existing plan-review sidecar schema; older sidecars without them simply fail the fallback preconditions (fail-safe).
- Rekey eligibility in remediation_mode_candidate with explicit two-branch control flow: (1) SIGNATURE PATH first, byte-identical to today - a valid certified plan-review manifest supplies material/SHA/regver baselines; (2) SIDECAR FALLBACK only when no valid plan-review manifest exists - baseline = the most recent REVIEW_RESULT sidecar; plan_changed compares the current material fingerprint against the sidecar's material_fingerprint; code_unchanged compares current_code_sha() against the sidecar's verified_at_sha; registry_unchanged compares the current registry version against the sidecar's regver. A sidecar missing any baseline field leaves that precondition False (never eligible on partial data). The existing early-return on signature-verification errors is replaced by fall-through to the sidecar branch; all other fail-safe behavior (any read error -> ineligible -> full review) is preserved.
- Docs: docs/plan-review-gate.md's eligibility description updated (replacing the honest-limitation paragraph added by task 67da of this epic).

## Scope
IN: two sidecar fields, the two-branch eligibility rekey, docs update, unit tests over the eligibility decision matrix, one integration test over a BLOCK loop. OUT: the drop math (novelty threshold/priority floor unchanged), the novelty scorer itself (task 0730 in this epic), drop permanence semantics, any change to the signature path's semantics when a valid manifest exists.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] With no certified signature but a prior sidecar carrying material_fingerprint + verified_at_sha + regver (plan changed, code + registry unchanged, within window), eligibility is TRUE. Oracle: unit test over remediation_mode_candidate with a fixture sidecar; `python -m pytest tests/unit -k "remediation" -x` green.
- [ ] With code drifted since the prior sidecar, OR a prior sidecar missing verified_at_sha or regver, eligibility is FALSE (fail-safe). Oracle: negative-case unit tests in the same module covering both.
- [ ] With a valid certified signature present, the decision is identical to today's signature path (the sidecar fallback is not consulted). Oracle: unit test asserting the signature-path result is unchanged for a signed fixture.
- [ ] A BLOCK-loop integration test (review -> BLOCK -> plan edit -> re-review) reaches the floor path (coverage.remediation.eligible true in the round-2 sidecar) without any signature present. Oracle: integration test asserting that field.
- [ ] docs/plan-review-gate.md's eligibility description matches the new behavior. Oracle: `grep -n "certified" docs/plan-review-gate.md` shows no claim that eligibility REQUIRES a certified signature.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` all pass.
